note: Famous thousand-torii shrine; the first section and lower shrines are paved and rollable (an elevator helps, open only 9am-3pm) with some steep ramps, but the full mountain torii climb is stairs and not accessible. See the iconic start.

The path to view the Golden Pavilion across the pond is packed gravel over solid ground - accessible (manual needs a little effort); free loaner chairs, disabled discount. The rest of the garden circuit has steps, so you turn back to exit.

Hillside temple with the famous wooden terrace; wheelchair accessible via a back entrance with ramps and free disabled entry, BUT the approach up Kiyomizu-zaka is a steep sloping street, hard for manual chairs. Ask staff for the accessible route.

The main bamboo path is smooth, paved and quite accessible with only gentle slopes - a rare easy roll through an iconic scene. Narrow and very crowded in spots. Surrounding shops are hit-or-miss for access.
Flat, straight accessible corridors make this UNESCO castle a good tourist spot for wheelchair users; some areas have steps but the main route works. Famous 'nightingale' floors.

Historic geisha district of wooden teahouses; some streets are pedestrian zones and rollable, but many old shops/restaurants have steps and stairs. Gion Corner theatre is wheelchair accessible. Atmospheric.

Iconic sloping preserved lanes toward Kiyomizu; Ninenzaka is partly accessible (you can get the Hokanji pagoda photo) but Sannenzaka is stairs and not accessible, and most little shops are tight with steps.
Iconic tower opposite Kyoto Station; reach the observation deck via elevators plus a sturdy stair-climber lift (staff-operated), then floor-to-ceiling city views. Convenient and accessible.

Famous Zen rock garden, but there are many stairs at the Sanmon main gate and wheelchairs cannot enter the temple/garden area - so the rock garden itself is not viewable if you can't walk. Grounds/pond partly rollable.
Modern aquarium near Kyoto Station; fully wheelchair accessible with wheelchair spaces for the dolphin show. A reliable, easy family option - great on a rainy day.
